solution:
```
from itertools import permutations
class Solution:
    def getMinSwaps(self, num: str, k: int) -> int:
        perms = sorted(list(set(permutations(num))))
        idx = perms.index(tuple(num))
        target = perms[idx + k]
        res = 0
        for i in range(len(num)):
            if num[i] != target[i]:
                j = i + 1
                while j < len(num) and num[j] != target[i]:
                    j += 1
                res += j - i
                num = list(num)
                num[i], num[j] = num[j], num[i]
                num = ''.join(num)
        return res
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2 * k)
space complexity: O(n * k)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution generates all permutations of the input string, finds the kth smallest wonderful integer, and then calculates the minimum number of adjacent digit swaps needed to reach it.
